text_content: No separate Design Output Review (DOR) will be performed for this project. An approval of the MRD revision after V&V completion shall confirm that the design output activities have passed and closed.  However, Design Transfer Reviews will be conducted as part of this project. To expedite NMPA submission timeline, this project plans to conduct two separate design transfer reviews for efficient utilization of time and resources. Hence, 1st DTR will be conducted prior to NMPA submission focusing on completing all mandatory deliverables and documents per NMPA requirements (including manufacturing deliverables) and 2nd DTR will be conducted post NMPA submission ensuring completion of all remaining deliverables. Refer to supporting document - HAL 4.0MR2 (HAL Evolve) project schedule detailing tasks under each DTR. text_content: Mitigations through product design and labeling are in place. The mitigations were derived from thorough risk analysis processes through which risk control measures are formulated, implemented, and validated during product development. The risk control measures have been tested through verification and validation activities, and through product usability studies. The risks and their associated potential hazards have been assessed via analysis of available clinical data regarding the use of HAL. The safety findings in the clinical data were analyzed in context of the benefits to the patients as described above. Clinical data was analyzed for any evidence of the residual risks of HAL 4.0 identified in the RMR report. No adverse events or clinical toxicities were reported as attributable to the residual risks outlined in Section 5.1.4 (e.g. radiation over/under exposure, collision).

text_content: QNotes processes. The issues that are found during testing are logged as DRs, and the impact to verification and validation testing is assessed. This assessment defines whether the part needs to be replaced or reworked in the machines after implementation of the design change. This decision is documented in the DR. The hardware changes are implemented, and the final material dispositioning (per DR) is done via EC process. Upgrades are performed per the information in ECs and DR and are documented in QNotes system. The DHR of this device is captured at the time of build, and before verification TRR. Validation Testing HAL SN3 will be used for Validation Testing after the Validation TRR passes. Additional QA checks for the machine configuration are performed by comparing the machine DHR to the final Halcyon specifications. Any differences in parts will have a “Use-As-Is” EC justification. source: D69533 HAL 4.0 MR1 Maintenance Release Document Rev09.docx.pdf
title: 5. Verification and Validation > 5.1 Verification and Validation Plan > 5.1.6 Verification and Validation Activities > 5.1.6.1 V&V Approach > 5.1.6.1.3 Regression Testing
text_content: Existing HAL test cases will be selected to run based on the impact analysis with regard to the changes made in HAL 4.0 MR1 including 4.0 MR1 development, DR fixes, merge with True Beam, and compatibility with updates to ARIA and Eclipse. All safety and risk controls will be tested as a part of verification unless justification is provided. Impact analysis for determining the verification Test Case and Test Description was performed via D71770 HAL 4.0 MR1 Verification TRR1 Impact Analysis [13] and D73115 HAL 4.0 MR1 Verification TRR2 Impact Analysis [14]. Regression analysis will be performed based on an opt in methodology, whereby requirements affected by HAL 4.0 MR1 will be identified along with a justification why regression testing is necessary. Linked test cases to those requirements will then be included in the HAL 4.0 MR1 regression test suite: all other HAL test cases will not be run for the HAL 4.0 MR1 release.

source: D44094_HAL4.0_CER_Rev05.pdf
title: 3. DAEN > 6.1.4 Risk Management Data
text_content: The risk data reported in the risk management report for the HAL were reviewed for this evaluation and compared to findings in the clinical data analyses. Potential effects of device hazards may impact patients, operators, or service personnel, and the environment in which the devices are used. These elements were evaluated as part of the risk assessment. Each of the residual risks are at the LOW and/or MEDIUM risk level. Each has been analyzed in the RMR in the context of risk-benefit ratio expected from the intended clinical use of the devices. This CER compares risk findings from clinical data to those identified in the RMR.

source: D40348 HAL 4.0 TSCL for SN 4 Release 4.00.0122 Rev14.pdf
title: 4.0 verification and validation testing.
text_content: Verification Testing Halcyon SN4 was built per Pre-production Phase I (Silver) DMR, and is maintained in the production equivalent configuration using Discrepancy Report (DR) Handling and Engineering Change (EC) and QNotes processes. Issues that are found during testing are logged as DRs, and the impact to verification and validation testing is assessed. This assessment defines whether the part needs to be replaced or reworked in the machines after implementation of the design change. This decision is documented in the DR. The hardware changes are implemented, and the final material dispositioning (per DR) is done via EC process. Upgrades are performed per the information in ECs and DR and are documented in QNotes system. The Device Parts List of the verification devices is captured at the time of build, and before verification TRR. Validation Testing Halcyon SN4 was upgraded to the final production equivalent configuration (defined by Gold DMR).

source: D23997 Halcyon v2 Design and Development Plan Rev 01.pdf
title: 3.2.1 Scope, Schedule and Budget Control
text_content: Scope control will consist of managing requirement changes, enhancements, feature requests and scope changes.  Each will be controlled as outlined below. Requirement changes: If all of these conditions are met:  The changes do not impact the essential functionality and/or user experience of the device  The changed do not impact the program schedule committed to MER by more than two calendar weeks Then the changes will be managed through the document control process using Review Report Forms (LL40). If the above conditions are not all met, then the requirement change is elevated to a change in scope by definition.  See Scope Change section below. Enhancements and Feature Requests: Any requirement changes to implement additional features or enhancements, after Elaboration Phase, will be collected as TFS backlog items reporting to an Enhancement EPIC. title: 5. Verification and Validation
text_content: Design verification and validation testing in not in scope for this project. Manufacturing will build machines to ensure manufacturing readiness as part of ECN implementation. Small tube/small imager (RTI4343L) configuration was tested with Ethos during HAL 4.0 project [3] [4] [5] [6]. Large tube/small imager (HiX4343L) configuration was fully tested with Halcyon 4.0 with ARIA. [3] [4] [7] [8]. Large tube/small imager does not impact Ethos radiotherapy system functionality with Ethos software. No change in user needs for both Halcyon and Ethos radiotherapy system because of large tube/small imager. Also, please refer D78172 HAL 4.0 MR1 Ethos Sanity Checks Using HiXRTI4343L Review Report Rev 01.

source: D65932 HAL4.0 IEC 60601-2-1 (2020) 201.10.101.5.3 - Deviation of Beam Characteristics Between Control Points Report Rev 01.pdf
title: 4. IEC Compliance Checks (Continued)
text_content: IRRADIATION. Repeat for each of the other two values. Methods A simple plan containing only two control points was loaded in Service Mode and delivered once without interruption. Then the plan was delivered again and during the delivery a change was made to the yield, off-axis intensity, or symmetry. Yield was changed by changing the HPVS, and off-axis intensity and symmetry were changed by changing the respective gain or balance values. The BGM log files were then examined to verify that the irradiation was stopped when the yield, off-axis intensity, or symmetry exceeded the allowed variation. This process was repeated to test the upper and lower bounds of all three values. title: 3. DAEN > 7.1.8 Conclusions of the Analysis of Manufacturer Data (Continued)
text_content: Mitigations through product design and labeling are in place. The mitigations were derived from thorough risk analysis processes through which risk control measures are formulated, implemented, and validated during product development. The risk control measures have been tested through verification and validation activities, and through product usability studies. The risks and their associated potential hazards have been assessed via analysis of available clinical data regarding the use of HAL. The safety findings in the clinical data were analyzed in context of the benefits to the patients as described above. Clinical data was analyzed for any evidence of the residual risks of HAL 4.0 identified in the RMR report. No adverse events or clinical toxicities were reported as attributable to the residual risks outlined in Section 5.1.4 (e.g. radiation over/under exposure, collision).

title: 3.0 Definitions > 4.2 Integrity > 4.2.2 Software Integrity:
text_content: During the design and development phases of the lifecycle for the application software within Varian medical devices, the integrity of the system build is ensured by controlling the editing process and requiring regular reviews of the code. This is done to ensure that the integrity of the application software is maintained through the construction phase. W hen the software package is ready for release to the user, Varian manufacturing procedures (DND1902) are used to prevent improper or incompatible versions of the software from being distributed or installed. These procedures include measures to ensure the consistency of data, the absence of viruses or malware, and the inclusion of needed user documentation and labeling. For Varian medical devices, installation procedures and updates are handled by trained Varian Service personnel or local Service representatives.
